hero.question: Will Roberto Sánchez Palomino win the 2026 Peruvian presidential election?
hero.prob_take: At 17.2%, the market is pricing Sánchez Palomino as a long-shot third or fourth tier candidate, which assumes Peru's political fragmentation won't consolidate around a single anti-establishment figure by 2026. The -10.5 point collapse in 24 hours suggests either new negative information or profit-taking, but watch whether his rural base holds firm when the campaign actually heats up.

daily_take.headline: Peru's Sánchez Palomino Collapses 10 Points. Market Pricing in a Done Deal?
daily_take.deck: The Peruvian frontrunner dropped from 27.2% to 17.2% in 24 hours on $5.7M volume, signaling serious news. Either the market knows something real about his candidacy or it's panic selling on thin liquidity. At 17.2%, you're getting a candidate who was competitive yesterday at 2x the price. Smart money checks the news, then buys the dip.
